questions.text: The chemical properties of elements depend on _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Atomic number refers to the number of protons present in the nucleus of an atom. It is denoted by the symbol "Z." The atomic number uniquely identifies an element and determines its position on the periodic table. In a neutral atom, the number of protons is equal to the number of electrons orbiting the nucleus. This balance between protons and electrons allows the atom to be electrically neutral since protons carry a positive charge, while electrons carry a negative charge. The atomic number is a fundamental property of an element and defines its chemical identity. For example, all atoms with an atomic number of 6 have six protons and are therefore carbon atoms. Elements are arranged on the periodic table in ascending order of their atomic numbers. The atomic number also determines other properties of an element, such as its position in the periodic table, the electronic configuration of its atoms, and its chemical behavior. Elements with different atomic numbers exhibit distinct chemical and physical properties due to variations in their atomic structure and electron configurations. In summary, the atomic number of an element represents the number of protons in its nucleus and is a key factor in defining its identity and characteristics.

Mass number refers to the total number of protons and neutrons present in the nucleus of an atom. It is denoted by the symbol "A." In an atom, the nucleus is composed of protons and neutrons, which are collectively known as nucleons. Protons have a positive charge, while neutrons are electrically neutral. Electrons, which are negatively charged particles, orbit the nucleus in electron shells but contribute very little to the mass of the atom. The mass number is an important property of an atom and provides information about its overall mass. It is determined by adding together the number of protons and neutrons in the nucleus. Mathematically, it can be expressed as: Mass number (A) = Number of protons (Z) + Number of neutrons (N) Since the mass of a proton and a neutron is roughly the same, the mass number can also be considered as an approximation of the total number of nucleons in the nucleus. For example, consider carbon, which has an atomic number (number of protons) of 6. Carbon-12 (C-12) has 6 protons and 6 neutrons, so its mass number is 12. Carbon-13 (C-13), on the other hand, has 6 protons and 7 neutrons, so its mass number is 13. Isotopes of an element have the same atomic number (Z) but different mass numbers (A) due to variations in the number of neutrons. Different isotopes of an element can have slightly different masses and may exhibit different nuclear stability and radioactive properties. In summary, the mass number represents the total number of protons and neutrons in the nucleus of an atom and helps distinguish between different isotopes of an element. Chemical properties depend only on the atomic number as it also shows the number of electrons which are the very subatomic particles that take part in a chemical reaction. Atomic masses influence only the physical properties.

Chemical properties depend only on the atomic number as it also shows the number of electrons which are the very subatomic particles that take part in a chemical reaction. Atomic masses influence only the physical properties.

options.text: Neither atomic number nor mass number
options.explanation: Chemical properties depend only on the atomic number as it also shows the number of electrons which are the very subatomic particles that take part in a chemical reaction. Atomic masses influence only the physical properties.

questions.explanation: The chemical properties of an atom depend upon the atomic number. From atomic numbers we can get valence electrons that determine the chemical properties of it.

questions.text: An element whose atomic number is same but mass number is different is called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This term is used for molecules generally. An isomer has the same molecular formula but different structural formula or orientation in space
Isobars are atoms of different chemical elements because they have a different number of protons. However, the total number of nucleons (protons + neutrons) is the same.

An element with the same atomic number but different mass numbers is called an "isotope." Isotopes of an element have the same number of protons (which determines the atomic number) but differ in the number of neutrons in the nucleus, leading to different mass numbers.
An isotone has the same number of neutrons.

questions.explanation: C is the answer as the atomic number is the proton number and the mass number is the nucleon number. Having the same proton number implies the atoms are of the same element so, having different nucleon numbers indicates a difference in the number of neutrons which is the characteristics of isotopes of the same element.

questions.text: What is the quark composition of a Proton?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Two up quarks and one down quark
Two up quarks and one strange quark
One up quark and two strange quarks
Two down quarks and one up quark

questions.explanation: Protons and Neutrons are made up of 3 quarks each. An up quark has charge +2/3 whereas a down quark has charge -2/3. Protons have charge +1therefore have 2 up quarks and one down quark: (2*2/3) + (1 * -1/3) = +1

questions.text: The half-life of a radioactive element depends upon:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: It does not depend on the temperature, concentration, or the amount of element and pressure, but just depends on the time factor.Hence, this is an incorrect option.
It does not depend on the temperature, concentration, or the amount of element and pressure, but just depends on the time factor.Hence, this is an incorrect option.
It does not depend on the temperature, concentration, or the amount of element and pressure, but just depends on the time factor.Hence, this is an incorrect option.

options.text: None of the above options are correct
options.explanation: The half-life period of a radioactive element is the time required for a quantity to fall to half its value as measured at the beginning of the time period. It does not depend on the temperature, concentration, or the amount of element, but just depends on the time factor.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The half-life of a radioactive element is a fundamental property of that element, determined by the stability of its nucleus, and does not change with external factors like the amount of element present, temperature, or pressure. Options A, B, and C are incorrect because they suggest that half-life could be influenced by these external conditions, which it is not.

questions.text: Radiation can cause:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Radiation exposure is a known risk factor for the development of leukemia. The bone marrow is highly sensitive to radiation, which can disrupt the normal production of blood cells and lead to this type of cancer.
Radiation sickness or acute radiation syndrome results from a high dose of radiation over a short period, leading to symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and potential organ damage.
Long-term exposure to ionizing radiation, especially UV radiation, can damage skin cell DNA, increasing the risk of skin cancer.
Ionizing radiation can directly damage DNA, causing mutations that may lead to various health issues, including cancer. These mutations can also be inherited if they occur in germ cells.
All of the above are potential consequences of radiation exposure, affecting different systems and leading to various health issues.

questions.explanation: Radiation can lead to multiple adverse health effects, including leukemia, radiation sickness, skin cancer, and gene mutations. Each of these outcomes stems from the ability of radiation to damage biological tissues and DNA. Leukemia is linked to radiation's impact on bone marrow, radiation sickness results from acute high-level exposure, skin cancer is associated with UV radiation, and gene mutations can occur due to ionizing radiation disrupting DNA. Therefore, the most comprehensive answer is all of the above, as radiation can indeed cause all these effects.

questions.text: A nucleus consists of 11 protons and 12 neutrons. The conventional symbol of nucleus is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: 11 protons are present in sodium(Na) not in calcium(Ca). The conventional symbol of the nucleus contains proton number(p) and mass number(p + n). So the answer is 11Na23, which is mentioned in option C.

11 protons are present in sodium(Na) not in calcium(Ca). The conventional symbol of the nucleus contains proton number(p) and mass number(p + n). So the answer is 11Na23, which is mentioned in option C.

questions.text: Fusion and fission reactions are associated with:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Water or hydal energy involves the movement of water in rivers or waterfalls to generate electricity, which is unrelated to nuclear reactions.
Geothermal energy harnesses the Earth's internal heat, which is different from the energy released in nuclear reactions.
Fossil fuel energy originates from the chemical energy stored in ancient organic matter, not from nuclear reactions.
Nuclear energy is produced through nuclear reactions, specifically fission and fusion, which involve changes in the atomic nucleus.
Tidal energy is derived from the gravitational effects of the moon and sun on Earth's oceans, not from nuclear reactions.

questions.explanation: Both fission and fusion are nuclear reactions that produce energy, but the applications are not the same. Fission is the splitting of a heavy, unstable nucleus into two lighter nuclei, and fusion is the process where two light nuclei combine together releasing vast amounts of energy.

questions.text: To remove the huge amount of heat energy in a nuclear reactor, _ is/are used.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The core is the part of the nuclear reactor where the nuclear fuel is contained and fission reactions take place, generating heat. However, it does not remove heat; it is the source of heat.
Control rods are used to manage the rate of the nuclear reaction by absorbing neutrons. They do not play a role in heat removal.
Shielding is used to protect the environment and personnel from radiation exposure, not for heat removal.
Moderators are used to slow down neutrons to sustain the chain reaction, but they do not remove heat from the reactor.
Coolants are fluids or gases that circulate through the reactor core to absorb and transfer the heat generated by nuclear fission, making them essential for heat removal.

questions.explanation: The heat released by fission in nuclear reactors must be captured and transferred for use in electricity generation. To this end, reactors use coolants that remove heat from the core where the fuel is processed.

questions.text: The half-life of C14 is about:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is incorrect. 1637 years is not a recognized half-life for carbon-14 or any common isotope used in dating.
This is incorrect. 892 years is not associated with the half-life of carbon-14.
This is incorrect. 10 years is far too short for the half-life of carbon-14, which is used in dating ancient artifacts.
This is incorrect. 100 years is not the half-life of carbon-14 and is not suitable for radiocarbon dating's time scale.

Correct. The half-life of carbon-14 is approximately 5730 years. It is widely used for dating organic materials up to about 50,000 years old, which is why this half-life is significant in archaeology and geology.

questions.explanation: Carbon-14 has a half-life of 5,730 ± 40 years—i.e. half the amount of the radioisotope present at any given time will undergo spontaneous disintegration during the succeeding 5,730 years.

questions.text: The detection and estimation of an element in a mixture is sometimes nearly impossible, if it is present in very minute traces or if it’s chemical properties are very similar to those of other elements in the mixture. An effective technique is developed for these purposes is known as?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Simple analysis methods are inadequate for detecting elements in very small amounts or when they have similar properties to others in the mixture. They usually involve basic, low-sensitivity techniques that aren't suitable for complex detection tasks.

Spectral analysis, such as spectroscopy, is useful for identifying elements based on their unique electromagnetic signatures. However, it may struggle with elements present in extremely low concentrations or those with overlapping spectra.

Activation analysis is specifically designed for detecting and estimating elements present in minute quantities. It involves irradiating the sample to make it radioactive, allowing for precise measurement of the elements based on their emitted radiation.
Geometric analysis pertains to the study of spatial properties and is unrelated to chemical detection methods. It doesn't apply to the detection of elements in mixtures.
Mechanical analysis focuses on the physical properties of materials, such as strength and elasticity, and does not involve chemical identification or estimation of elements in a mixture.

questions.explanation: Activation analysis is a highly sensitive technique used to detect and measure trace elements in a mixture, especially when they are present in very small amounts or have similar chemical properties to other elements. By irradiating the sample, it becomes radioactive, and the subsequent measurement of emitted radiation allows for precise identification and quantification of the elements. Other methods like simple analysis lack the sensitivity required, while spectral analysis may face challenges with low concentrations or similar spectra. Geometric and mechanical analyses are unrelated to chemical detection.

questions.text: The penetration power of β-particle as compared to α—particle is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option suggests that β-particles have only 10 times more penetration power than α-particles, which is not accurate. In reality, β-particles have a much higher penetration power than α-particles.
This option suggests that β-particles have 100 times less penetration power than α-particles, which is not accurate. β-particles can penetrate matter more effectively than α-particles.
This is the correct option. β-particles have about 100 times more penetration power compared to α-particles. They can penetrate matter more deeply and are less easily stopped by materials.
This option suggests that β-particles have only 10 times less penetration power than α-particles, which is not accurate. In reality, β-particles have a much higher penetration power than α-particles.

questions.explanation: The range of the β—particle is 100 times more than that of α—particles. But its ionizing ability is about 100 times less than that of α—particle.

questions.text: An α-particle can produce fluorescence in:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: ZnS is a commonly used phosphor material in scintillation detectors and is sensitive to α-particles. When an α-particle interacts with ZnS, it can produce visible light, leading to fluorescence.
Sodium iodide crystals are another common scintillator material used for detecting α-particles. When an α-particle interacts with sodium iodide, it can also produce scintillation or fluorescence.
Barium Palatino Cyanide is another common scintillator material used for detecting α-particles.

An α-particle can produce fluorescence in various materials, and all of the given options (ZnS, sodium iodide, and barium platinocyanide) are known for their scintillation properties when interacting with α-particles.

questions.explanation: Charged particles α or β and γ—radiation produce fluorescence or glow on striking some substance like zinc sulfide, sodium iodide, or barium platinocyanide coated screens.

questions.text: The path of β-particle is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: They do not follow a straight-line path like α-particles (alpha particles) do.

β-particles are high-energy electrons (β-) or positrons (β+) that are emitted during certain types of radioactive decay. They do not follow a straight-line path like α-particles (alpha particles) do. The zig-zag or erratic path of β-particles is a result of their interactions with other atoms and electrons in the medium through which they are traveling. These interactions cause the β-particles to scatter and change direction, leading to their characteristic zig-zag or erratic trajectory.
β-particles do not show such a trajectory. They show zig-zag path.
β-particles do not show such a trajectory. They show zig-zag path.

questions.explanation: β —particles are more easily deflected by collisions than heavy α—particles. Thus the path of β-particles in matter is not straight but shows much straggling or scattering.

questions.text: Which of the following will be a better shield against 𝛾-rays?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. While water can attenuate gamma rays, a very large thickness is required to provide effective shielding.
This option is incorrect. Although heavy water has a slightly higher density than ordinary water, it is still not as effective as lead in shielding gamma rays.
This option is correct. Lead is an excellent shield against gamma rays due to its high density and atomic number, which effectively absorb the radiation.
This option is incorrect. Aluminum has a lower density compared to Lead, making it less effective in shielding against gamma rays.

questions.explanation: Gamma rays are known to have maximum penetration they can penetrate several metals. The best shielding against them is elements with high atomic numbers and high density, such as lead. The atomic number of Lead is 82.Gamma rays travel at the speed of light, and they can travel thousands of meters in the air before spending their energy. They can be shielded by water but a huge amount would be required: 13.8 feet of water, about 6.6 feet of concrete, or about 1.3 feet of Lead is required to shield against gamma rays. Aluminium is less dense and gamma rays can penetrate through them completely.

questions.text: Which one of the following possesses maximum velocity?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Alpha rays (α): Alpha particles are composed of two protons and two neutrons, which makes them relatively massive and positively charged. Due to their larger mass and charge, they have relatively low velocities. They typically move at speeds of around 5% the speed of light.

Gamma rays (γ): Gamma rays are electromagnetic radiation, similar to X-rays, and they are composed of high-energy photons. They move at the speed of light (approximately 299,792,458 meters per second in a vacuum), which is the maximum velocity possible for anything in the universe.

Beta rays (β): Beta particles are high-energy electrons (beta-minus) or positrons (beta-plus). They have higher velocities compared to alpha particles, but they are still slower than gamma rays. Beta particles can move at various speeds depending on their kinetic energy, which can vary from a few percent to nearly the speed of light.

options.text: All of the above have same speed
options.explanation: Among the options given, gamma rays have the maximum velocity because they travel at the speed of light, which is faster than both alpha and beta particles.

questions.explanation: Gamma rays have the maximum velocity because they travel at the speed of light, which is faster than both alpha and beta particles.
